data.content:
Here's the full diagnosis:

---

## Build Failure Analysis — `BuildDiag.sln`

### Solution structure
```
Core.csproj        (library, no dependencies)         ✅ OK
Services.csproj    (library, depends on Core)          ❌ FAILED
Api.csproj         (exe, depends on Services + Core)   ✅ OK (didn't compile — skipped due to Services failure)
```

### Root Cause (1 of 1) — `Services.csproj`

**Missing NuGet package: `Newtonsoft.Json`**

`Services/UserService.cs` uses `Newtonsoft.Json` (`JObject`, etc.) but `Services.csproj` has **no `<PackageReference>` for it**. The only dependency declared is a `<ProjectReference>` to `Core`.

All 3 errors are the same root cause:

| Error | Location | Message |
|-------|----------|---------|
| CS0246 | UserService.cs(2,7) | `using Newtonsoft.Json;` — namespace not found |
| CS0246 | UserService.cs(3,7) | `using Newtonsoft.Json.Linq;` — namespace not found |
| CS0246 | UserService.cs(22,19) | `JObject` type not found |

### Cascading failures
- **`Api.csproj`** — has zero compiler errors of its own. It was blocked from building only because its dependency `Services.csproj` failed. Once Services is fixed, Api will build.
- **`Core.csproj`** — built successfully with no issues.

### Fix
Add the missing NuGet reference to `Services/Services.csproj`:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
</ItemGroup>
```

Then `dotnet restore && dotnet build` should succeed.
